title: westringia grandifolia
intro: Westringia grandifolia, commonly known as the coastal rosemary, is a shrub native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Westringia grandifolia include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. Westringia grandifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for different purposes.
